Home > KMS > KMS ver. 1.2.410 – MapleStory Crown: Kinesis Remaster!

KMS ver. 1.2.410 – MapleStory Crown: Kinesis Remaster!

December 28, 2025 Max Leave a comment Go to comments

The first patch of the MapleStory Crown update has been released! The main features include the Kinesis remaster, tons various game improvements and quality of life changes, and Challenge World season 3. This time, there’s an extra Challenge World-exclusive season t, Kai!

Sorry for the post being late, this patch came out a week and a half ago but since there were only a few days inbetween the KMST and KMS release, I didn’t have enough time to do a KMST post (as I mentioned before) so I had to translate everything all at once (and there was a lot in these patch notes). Then I was busy playing the actual game and other assorted holiday stuff lol.

Wishing everyone a Merry Christmas, happy holidays, and happy new year!

Overview

  • Kinesis Remaster
    • Common
    • Quests

      • [Custom Illustration] Colourful Energy
    • Little Snowman’s Happiness
    • Sunday Maple
    • Event Improvements

Kinesis Remaster

Common

  • Psychic Points have been removed.
  • MP has been added.
  • The Hyper Stat for ‘DF/TP/PP Increase’ has been changed to ‘DF/TF Increase’.
  • The character creation UI’s illustration has been changed.
  • The character select UI’s job-exclusive emblem has been changed.
  • The Creed Hair/Cliff Hair have been added.
  • The Kinesis Face has been added.
  • The following items have been added.
    • Preppy King
    • Preppy Queen
    • White Sneakers
  • The skill voices have been changed.

[Back to Overview]

Quests

  • The ‘People in Need of a Hero’ quests have been streamlined into a single location in Riena Strait.
  • The ‘People in Need of a Hero’ quest pre-requisite condition has been removed from the ‘Yuna’s Concern’ quest.
  • The ‘Weaker, Stronger’/’Nero’s Training Plan 4’ quests have been removed.
  • The ‘Jay’s Secret Folder’ quest has been added.
  • Certain quests’ cutscenes have been improved.
  • Navigation has been added to certain quests.

[Back to Overview]

Skills

Beginner

  • Psychic Attack
    • This skill has been changed into a skill that consumes MP.
    • The maximum number of enemies hit has been increased from 1 to 3.
    • The number of hits has been increased from 1 to 2.
    • The action delay has been decreased by about 17%.
    • The skill effect has been changed.
    • The skill icon has been changed.
    • The skill sound effects have been changed.
    • The feature that charged Psychic Points has been removed.
  • Return
    • The skill effect has been changed.
    • The skill icon has been changed.
    • The skill sound effects have been changed.
    • The action delay has been decreased by about 45%.

[Back to Overview]

1st Job

  • Psychic Force
    [Master Level: 20]
    Push enemies back with telekinesis. You can use this skill with the down arrow key to push enemies downwards. Certain monsters will resist being pushed.
    • [Level 20]
      Consumes 10 MP. Deals 110% damage 3 times on up to 5 enemies.
  • ESP Booster
    [Master Level: 10]
    Increases your attack speed for a certain period of time.
    Can be registered in a Skill Sequence.
    • [Level 10]
      Consumes 20 MP. For 180 seconds, increases attack speed by 2 stages.
  • Psychic Walk
    [Master Level: 5]
    Jump once more while in the air to move a certain distance. You can use this skill up to 2 times. Youc an use this skill with the up arrow key to jump upwards.
    • [Level 5]
      Consumes 5 MP. Jump a certain distance.
  • Defense
    [Master Level: 10]
    Surround your body with pure telekinetic power to defend against enemies’ attacks and increase your endurance.
    • [Level 10]
      20% max HP, 30% max MP, 500 defense, 75% damage taken reduction.
  • Inherent
    [Master Level: 20]
    The psychic powers imprinted on your cells enhance your body.
    • [Level 20]
      20 speed, 20 maximum speed, 20 jump, 15% critical rate.

[Back to Overview]

2nd Job

  • Psychic Lift
    [Master Level: 20]
    Lift enemies and slam them into a force field of high-density telekinetic power.
    • [Level 20]
      Consumes 20 MP. Deals 220% damage 3 times on up to 6 enemies.
  • Ultimate: Pressure
    [Master Level: 20]
    Powerful telekinetic power distorts space to create a Micro Black Hole.
    If the Micro Black Hole hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage. It will prioritize being created at the location of the boss monster with the highest max HP.
    Micro Black Hole’s reactivation cooldown is not affected by cooldown reset effects.
    • [Level 20]
      When Kinesis uses a directly attacking skill (except common skills), there is a 100% chance to activate a Micro Black Hole at the location of an enemy within a certain range.
      Micro Black Hole: Activate 6 gravitational waves that deal 210% damage 4 times on up to 8 enemies. A single enemy can only be hit by up to 4 attacks.
      Reactivation cooldown: 6 seconds.
      Unleash reactivation cooldown: 2 seconds.
  • Psychic Rush
    [Master Level: 1]
    Use telekinetic power to push your body and quickly rush forwards.
    • [Level 1]
      Consumes 100 MP. Rush a certain distance.
  • ESP Mastery
    [Master Level: 20]
    Gain mastery over the ESP Limiter.
    • [Level 20]
      50% ESP Limiter mastery, 40 INT, 30 magic attack.
  • Pure Power
    [Master Level: 20]
    Awaken your abilities with the pure energy of telekinetic power.
    • [Level 20]
      1 attack speed stage, 10% damage, 15% critical rate.
  • Inherent II
    [Master Level: 13]
    The psychic powers imprinted on your cells further enhance your body.
    • [Level 13]
      10% final damage, 50% stance.

[Back to Overview]

3rd Job

  • Psychic Crash
    [Master Level: 20]
    Instantly lift enemies into the air with telekinetic power then slam them into the ground.
    • [Level 20]
      Consumes 20 MP. Deals 300% damage 4 times on up to 6 enemies.
  • Ultimate: Opening
    [Master Level: 20]
    Maximize the universal law of gravitation to open up the prelude of battle.
    Inflict a debuff on the enemy with the highest max HP within range. A powerful gravitational field will be formed around the debuffed enemy, pulling in objects and attacking around it.
    If any of Ultimate: Opening’s attacks (other than the first attack) hit an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage. This skill is not affected by cooldown reset effects.
    • [Level 20]
      Consumes 200 MP. Deals 260% damage 6 times on 1 enemy, then inflicts a debuff on that enemy for 7 seconds.
      Objects will be pulled towards the debuffed enemy, activating up to 2 gravitational fields that deal 260% damage 6 times on up to 8 enemies.
      When the debuff duration ends, it will explode, dealing 390% damage 12 times.
      If you use Opening while there is an enemy inflicted with Opening, the previous Opening will instantly end and activate the explosion, with additional attacks equal to the number of remaining gravitational field attacks.
      Cooldown: 30 seconds.
      Unleash cooldown: 10 seconds.
  • Psychic Move
    [Master Level: 4]
    Ride a wave of telekinetic power and walk freely through the sky.
    You can move in up to 5 directions by combining the left/right/up arrow keys. If you do not input a directional key, you will float in place.
    Psychic Move is not affected by cooldown reset effects.
    • [Level 4]
      Consumes 100 MP. Fly for a certain period of time.
      Cooldown: 2 seconds.
  • Warp
    [Master Level: 5]
    Gather and condense vast amounts of telekinetic power to warp space. You can teleport through the warped space.
    • [Level 5]
      Consumes 55 MP and 1 Condensed Telekinetic Power.
      Teleport 570px horizontally or 320px vertically.
      You prepare 1 Condensed Telekinetic Power every 10 seconds, and you can store up to 3.
  • Telekinesis
    [Master Level: 15]
    When Kinesis’ directly attacking skills hit an enemy, compressed energy deals an additional attack on enemies.
    Telekinesis will prioritize attacking the boss monster with the highest max HP. If it hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage.
    • [Level 15]
      When Kinesis’ directly attacking skills (except for common skills) hit an enemy, there is a 50% chance to create 3 Compressed Energy Bullets. Ultimate skills have a 40% chance to create them.
      Energy Bullet: Deals 110% damage 3 times.
  • Mental Enhancement
    [Master Level: 20]
    Clear your mind to gain higher concentration in battle.
    • [Level 20]
      20 magic attack, 16% damage, 15% final damage.
  • Transcendence
    [Master Level: 20]
    Through supernatural powers, transcend your regular body’s abilities.
    • [Level 20]
      30 magic attack, 10% critical damage.
  • Alleviate
    [Master Level: 20]
    Surround your body with precisely controlled telekinetic power to enhance your resistance to external attacks.
    • [Level 20]
      20 status resistance, 30% elemental resistance, 50% stance, 30% max HP, 20% chance to avoid attacks.

[Back to Overview]

4th Job

  • Psychic Smashing
    [Master Level: 30]
    Lift an otherworldly object and smash it into enemies.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 40 MP. Deals 451% damage 5 times on up to 8 enemies.
      Enemies hit will be debuffed for 60 seconds, decreasing their defense by 15% and speed by 30%.
  • Ultimate: Gambit
    [Master Level: 30]
    Crash an empty train at high speeds to turn the tides of battle in a decisive move.
    If Ultimate: Gambit hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage. This skill is not affected by cooldown reset effects.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 200 MP. Activate 9 collisions that deal 147% damage 9 times on up to 12 enemies.
      Cooldown: 30 seconds.
      Unleash cooldown: 10 seconds.
  • Ultimate: Check
    [Master Level: 30]
    Condense a massive amount of energy into spheres and send them flying to threaten enemies.
    If Ultimate: Check hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage. This skill is not affected by cooldown reset effects.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 200 MP. Fire 5 energy spheres that deal 687% damage 7 times on up to 8 enemies.
      A single enemy can only be hit by up to 3 energy spheres.
      Cooldown: 30 seconds.
      Unleash cooldown: 10 seconds.
  • Unleash
    [Master Level: 30]
    Temporarily unlock the ESP Limiter, explosively releasing your suppressed telekinetic power. The strong supernatural powers drastically enhance the power of your Ultimate skills.
    Unleash is not affected by buff duration increasing effects or cooldown reset effects.
    Can be registered in a Skill Sequence.
    • [Level 30]
      When used, all Ultimate skills’ cooldowns are instantly reset.
      For 30 seconds, Ultimate skills’ final damage is increased by 12% and they will apply their Unleash cooldowns instead of their regular cooldowns.
      Cooldown: 120 seconds.
  • Other World’s Warrior
    [Master Level: 30]
    The Other World’s Goddess’ blessing increases all of your stats by a certain percentage. When used, the Other World’s Goddess will temporarily appear.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 70 MP. When used, the Other World’s Goddess will appear.
      [Passive Effect: 15% increase to all stats of directly invested AP]
  • Mental Purification
    [Master Level: 5]
    Purify your mind to remove status effects. After using this skill, you will be immune to status effects for 3 seconds. However, this will not apply to certain status effects. Combat Orders does not apply to this skill.
    You can use Mental Purification while using other skills. In addition, permanently increases status resistance.
    • [Level 5]
      Consumes 30 MP. Cooldown: 180 seconds.
      [Passive Effect: 21 status resistance]
  • ESP Expert
    [Master Level: 30]
    Completely master your telekinetic power and ESP Limiter.
    Required Skill: Level 20 ESP Limiter.
    • [Level 30]
      73% ESP Limiter mastery, 73 magic attack, 10% final damage.
  • Awakening
    [Master Level: 20]
    Awaken as a true telekinetic.
    • [Level 20]
      9% magic attack, 26% final damage, 15% critical damage.
  • Mental Concentration
    [Master Level: 20]
    Unleash high levels of concentration to increase the effectiveness of your attacks.
    • [Level 20]
      20% buff duration, 18% final damage.
      When your attacks hit an enemy, absorb 5% max MP (3% max HP on boss monsters).
  • Criticality
    [Master Level: 30]
    Push all of your abilities to their limits and reach criticality.
    • [Level 30]
      20% final damage, 10% critical damage, 40% defense ignore.

[Back to Overview]

Hyper Skills

  • Psychic Smashing – Reinforce
    [Master Level: 1]
    Psychic Smashing’s damage is increased.
    • [Level 1]
      20% damage.
  • Psychic Smashing – Ignore Guard
    [Master Level: 1]
    Psychic Smashing’s defense ignore is increased.
    • [Level 1]
      20% defense ignore.
  • Psychic Smashing – Critical Rate
    [Master Level: 1]
    Psychic Smashing’s critical rate is increased.
    • [Level 1]
      20% critical rate.
  • Ultimate – Reinforce
    [Master Level: 1]
    Ultimate skills’ damage is increased.
    • [Level 1]
      20% damage.
  • Ultimate – Ignore Guard
    [Master Level: 1]
    Ultimate skills’ defense ignore is increased.
    • [Level 1]
      20% defense ignore.
  • Ultimate – Boss Killer
    [Master Level: 1]
    Ultimate skills’ boss damage is increased.
    • [Level 1]
      20% boss damage.
  • Telekinesis – Reinforce
    [Master Level: 1]
    Telekinesis’ damage is increased.
    • [Level 1]
      20% damage.
  • Telekinesis – Ignore Guard
    [Master Level: 1]
    Telekinesis’ defense ignore is increased.
    • [Level 1]
      20% defense ignore.
  • Telekinesis – Critical Rate
    [Master Level: 1]
    Telekinesis’ critical rate is increased.
    • [Level 1]
      20% critical rate.
  • Ever Psychic
    [Master Level: 1]
    Create a wave of powerful telekinetic power to ignore all damage for a certain period of time.
    • [Level 1]
      Consumes 150 MP. 10 seconds of invincibility.
      Cooldown: 120 seconds.
  • Psychic Over
    [Master Level: 1]
    Reverse your mental gravity to maximize your inner psychic power.
    Can be registered in a Skill Sequence.
    • [Level 1]
      Consumes 100 MP. For 60 seconds, 10% damage.
      Cooldown: 120 seconds.
  • Psychometry
    [Master Level: 1]
    Trace your enemies’ memories and make them relive their most painful moment to bind them.
    • [Level 1]
      Consumes 200 MP. Deals 700% damage 6 times on up to 15 enemies.
      Enemies hit are bound for 10 seconds. The bind duration is increased by up to 100% based on the damage that Psyhometry deals to the enemy.
      Cooldown: 120 seconds.

[Back to Overview]

5th Job

  • Psychic Bullet
    [Master Level: 30]
    Read the flow of particles in space then shoot telekinetic power in the opposite direction of the flow to tear space apart.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 1000 MP.
      Deals 200% damage 11 times on up to 15 enemies.
      When attacking normal monsters, increases damage by 415%p.
  • Psychic Nova
    [Master Level: 30]
    Summon a giant otherworldly object then compress it to the limit with telekinetic power. When the object reaches its critical point, it will explode and release powerful psychic energy to attack enemies.
    If Psychic Nova hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage.
    Can be registered in a Skill Sequence.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 1000 MP.
      Activate 12 compressions that deal 1635% damage 7 times on up to 15 enemies.
      When the object reaches its critical point, it will explode, activating 5 shockwaves that deal 1720% damage 14 times on up to 15 enemies.
      Cooldown: 120 seconds.
  • Ultimate: Checkmate
    [Master Level: 30]
    Manipulate otherworldly objects, sending them flying in unpredictable trajectories to attack enemies. A flawless, perfectly calculated sequence of attacks will end the battle.
    Ultimate: Checkmate will prioritize the boss monster with the highest max HP. If this skill hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage. This skill is not affected by cooldown reset effects.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 500 MP.
      Create 3 chess pieces that deal 437% damage 7 times on up to 12 enemies.
      The chess pieces will bounce up to 5 times towards enemies.
      During Unleash’s duration, when this attack hits an enemy, your directly used Ultimate skills’ cooldowns will be reset.
      Cooldown: 30 seconds.
      Unleash cooldown: 5 seconds.
  • Psychic Board
    [Master Level: 30]
    Completely control your enemies’ minds, creating a giant chess game on the battlefield.
    Open the game and designate kings to attack. During the game, Skewer will be activated at certain intervals. When your directly used Ultimate skills or Another Realm hit enemies a certain number of times, the game will end and an additional attack will be dealt to the designated kings.
    Psychic Board will prioritize attacking the boss monster with the highest max HP. If this skill htis an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage.
    Colour Cutscene On/Off: Mouse right-click.
    Can be registered in a Skill Sequence.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 1000 MP.
      Create a giant chess board and for 20 seconds, designate up to 15 enemies as kings.
      During the skill duration, for 5 seconds, Skewer will be activated at certain intervals.
      When this attack hits an enemy, every 1 second, Skewer’s activation time will be increased by 5 seconds. If Skewer’s activation time ends, it will no longer activate even for the rest of the buff duration.
      When your directly used Ultimate skills or Another Realm hit an enemy 75 times, the game will end and End Game will be activated as an additional attack on the designated kings.
      If End Game activates while Skewer had a remaining number of activations, activate additional hits equal to the number of remaining activations.
      Skewer: Deals 1540% damage 7 times on up to 15 enemies. Can be activated up to 28 times.
      End Game: Activate 5 endings that deal 1800% damage 11 times on the enemies designated as kings.
      Cooldown: 120 seconds.
  • Other World’s Afterimage
    [Master Level: 30]
    Open a portal that reflects the appearance of another world. The portal forces otherworldly forms to reflect on Maple World’s monsters.
    The otherworldly power flowing through the portal enhances Kinesis’ telekinetic power and restores their body.
    Can be registered in a Skill Sequence.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 100 HP. For 50 seconds, create the Other World’s Afterimage.
      When the Other World’s Afterimage is in the map, increases all stats of directly invested AP by 35% and critical damage by 5%.
      When the monster attacks, there is a 10% chance to recover 3% max HP and 3% max MP.
      Cooldown: 60 seconds.

[Back to Overview]

6th Job

  • Psychic Smashing VI
    [Master Level: 30]
    Lift an otherworldly object and smash it into enemies.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 50 MP. Deals 727% damage 5 times on up to 8 enemies.
      Enemies hit will be debuffed for 60 seconds, decreasing their defense by 15% and speed by 30%.
  • Ultimate: Pressure VI
    [Master Level: 30]
    Powerful telekinetic power distorts space to create a Micro Black Hole.
    If the Micro Black Hole hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage. It will prioritize being created at the location of the boss monster with the highest max HP.
    Micro Black Hole’s reactivation cooldown is not affected by cooldown reset effects.
    • [Level 30]
      When Kinesis uses a directly attacking skill (except common skills), there is a 100% chance to activate a Micro Black Hole at the location of an enemy within a certain range.
      Micro Black Hole: Activate 6 gravitational waves that deal 428% damage 4 times on up to 8 enemies. A single enemy can only be hit by up to 4 attacks.
      Reactivation cooldown: 6 seconds.
      Unleash reactivation cooldown: 2 seconds.
  • Ultimate: Check VI
    [Master Level: 30]
    Condense a massive amount of energy into spheres and send them flying to threaten enemies.
    If Ultimate: Check hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage. This skill is not affected by cooldown reset effects.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 200 MP. Fire 5 energy spheres that deal 1175% damage 7 times on up to 8 enemies.
      A single enemy can only be hit by up to 3 energy spheres.
      Cooldown: 30 seconds.
      Unleash cooldown: 10 seconds.
  • Ultimate: Opening VI
    [Master Level: 30]
    Maximize the universal law of gravitation to open up the prelude of battle.
    Inflict a debuff on the enemy with the highest max HP within range. A powerful gravitational field will be formed around the debuffed enemy, pulling in objects and attacking around it.
    If any of Ultimate: Opening’s attacks (other than the first attack) hit an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage. This skill is not affected by cooldown reset effects.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 1 MP. Deals 530% damage 6 times on 1 enemy, then inflicts a debuff on that enemy for 7 seconds.
      Objects will be pulled towards the debuffed enemy, activating up to 2 gravitational fields that deal 530% damage 6 times on up to 8 enemies.
      When the debuff duration ends, it will explode, dealing 796% damage 12 times.
      If you use Opening while there is an enemy inflicted with Opening, the previous Opening will instantly end and activate the explosion, with additional attacks equal to the number of remaining gravitational field attacks.
      Cooldown: 30 seconds.
      Unleash cooldown: 10 seconds.
  • Ultimate: Gambit VI
    [Master Level: 30]
    Crash an empty train at high speeds to turn the tides of battle in a decisive move.
    If Ultimate: Gambit hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage. This skill is not affected by cooldown reset effects.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 200 MP. Activate 9 collisions that deal 281% damage 9 times on up to 12 enemies.
      Cooldown: 30 seconds.
      Unleash cooldown: 10 seconds.
  • Telekinesis VI
    [Master Level: 30]
    When Kinesis’ directly attacking skills hit an enemy, compressed energy deals an additional attack on enemies.
    Telekinesis will prioritize attacking the boss monster with the highest max HP. If it hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage.
    • [Level 30]
      When Kinesis’ directly attacking skills (except for common skills) hit an enemy, there is a 50% chance to create 3 Compressed Energy Bullets. Ultimate skills have a 40% chance to create them.
      Energy Bullet: Deals 187% damage 3 times.
      [Passive Effect: Psychic Smashing VI’s damage is increased by 251%p.]
  •  Another Realm
    [Origin]
    [Master Level: 30]
    Drop an object from another world through a rift of warped space to destroy the area around you.
    During Unleash’s duration, the released psychic power enhances this skill’s damage.
    Level 10: 20% defense ignore.
    Level 20: 20% boss damage.
    Level 30: 30% defense ignore, 30% boss damage.
    • [Level 30]
      Consumes 1500 MP. You are invincible during the cast action.
      During Unleash’s duration, this skill’s final damage is increased by 8%.
      Activate 32 rifts that deal 1103% damage 12 times on up to 15 enemies, then activate 44 explosions that deal 1063% damage 13 times.
      Cooldown: 360 seconds.
  •  Fractal Horizon
    [Ascent]
    [Master Level: 30]
    Manipulate otherworldly matter replicated infinitely in a fractal structure to sweep away your surroundings.
    Level 10: 10% defense ignore, 10% boss damage.
    Level 20: 10% defense ignore, 10% boss damage.
    Level 30: 20% defense ignore.
    • [Level 30]
      You are invincible during the cast action.
      Activate 75 shockwaves that deal 1554% damage 15 times on up to 15 enemies.
      Additional 60% defense ignore, 40% boss damage, 100% critical rate.

[Back to Overview]

V Core Changes

  • The Psychic Force enhancement core has been removed.
  • The Psychic Crash enhancement core has been removed.
  • The Mad Crash enhancement core has been removed.
  • The Ultimate – Deep Impact enhancement core has been removed.
  • The Psychic Drain enhancement core has been removed.
  • The Psychic Grab enhancement core has been removed.
  • The Ultimate – Train enhancement core has been removed.
  • The Ultimate – B.P.M enhancement core has been removed.
  • The Everpsychic enhancement core has been removed.
  • The Psychic Force/Psychic Lift/Psychic Crash enhancement core has been added.
  • The Ultimate: Pressure/Ultimate: Gambit enhancement core has been added.
  • The Psychic Smashing enhancement core has been added.
  • The Ultimate: Check/Ultimate: Opening enhancement core has been added.
  • The Telekinesis enhancement core’s icon and core description have been changed.
  • The Psychometry enhancement core’s icon and core description have been changed.

[Back to Overview]

HEXA Core Changes

  • The Ultimate – Material VI core has been changed into the Ultimate: Pressure VI/Ultimate: Check VI core.
  • The Psychic Grab VI/Ultimate – Psychic Shot VI core has been changed into the Psychic Smashing VI core.
  • The Ultimate – Train VI/Ultimate – B.P.M VI core has been changed into the Ultimate: Opening VI/Ultimate: Gambit VI core.
  • The Psychic Tornado enhancement core has been changed into the Ultimate: Checkmate enhancement core.
  • The Ultimate – Moving Matter enhancement core has been changed into the Psychic Nova enhancement core.
  • The Ultimate – Psychic Bullet enhancement core has been changed into the Psychic Bullet enhancement core.
  • The Law of Gravity enhancement core has been changed into the Psychic Board enhancement core.

[Back to Overview]

Character Settings Change Support

For players whose character setting efficiencies have changed due to the Kinesis remaster, we are providing support to make it easy to change their HEXA Core enhancements and Ability settings.

Support Targets:

  • Kinesis characters created before December 18, 2025 at 12:00 AM.

Support Period:

  • December 18, 2025 after the maintenance ~ January 14, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

You can talk to Maple Admin through Quick Move or in major towns to receive the support items (can be claimed 1 time per character).

When you use the Change Circulator with a Legendary rank Ability, you can change a specific Ability’s stats to another Ability stat.

※ If the Ability rank is Unique or lower, you cannot use the Change Circulator.

※ The Ability level will be changed to the same skill level as the Ability you currently have.

※ If you do not have the Ability stat that is being changed, you cannot use the Change Circulator.

The stats that can be changed and the stats after the change are as follows.

※ If you change your ‘Boss Damage’ stat, you can choose one of ‘Magic Attack’, ‘Buff Duration’, or ‘Critical Rate’ to change the stat to.

[Back to Overview]

Growth Path Reorganization

Epic Dungeon

  • All experience rewards have been increased by about 34%.

Monster Park

  • Monster Park Extreme’s experience rewards have been increased as follows for level 275+ players.
    • Level 275~279: About 1~4% increase
    • Level 280~284: About 7~11% increase
    • Level 285~289: About 25~29% increase
    • Level 290+: About 45%~49% increase
  • New areas have been added to the Monster Park Advanced Dungeons.
    • Shangri-La (76.6b experience)
    • Arteria (107.2b experience)
    • Carcion (156.0b experience)
    • Tallahart (218.6b experience)
  • Monster Park’s Basic/Intermediate/Advanced Dungeons’ entry limits have been reorganized.
    • The daily clear limit per world has been increased from 7 to 14.
    • The daily free clears per world have been increased from 1 to 2.
    • When using the MVP Quick Pass, it has been improved so that it no longer reduces the number of free clears.
    • The daily clear limit per character will remain at 7.

Azmoth Canyon

  • All experience rewards have been increased by about 34%.

[Back to Overview]

Map Reorganization

Certain maps in the following Arcane River and Grandis areas have had their monster placements and terrain changed.

  • Road of Vanishing
  • Reverse City
  • Chew Chew Island
  • Yum Yum Island
  • City of Dreams Lacheln
  • Mysterious Forest Arcana
  • Swamp of Memories Morass
  • Primal Sea Esfera
  • Tenebris
  • Cernium
  • Hotel Arcs

Certain maps that you can move to through the Maple Guide have been added or changed.

[Back to Overview]

Authentic Symbol Effect Reorganization

When you reach level 11 on an Authentic Symbol or Grand Authentic Symbol, they will now provide the following additional effects.

[Back to Overview]

Link Skill Expansion

Link Skills’ master levels have been increased. The effects at master level are as follows.

You can increase your Link Skills’ master levels by using the new ‘Proof of a Radiant Hero’ item.

  • You can acquire a ‘Proof of a Radiant Hero’ by reaching level 285, completing the Carcion story quests, then proceeding with the related quest.
  • You can only acquire the ‘Proof of a Radiant Hero’ once per character.
  • The ‘Proof of a Radiant Hero’ can only be used by characters level 200 or higher that have completed the ‘[5th Job] Another Power, Arcane Force’ quest.
  • The ‘Proof of a Brilliant Hero’ can only be put into storage.

The following quests have been added to acquire the ‘Proof of a Brilliant Hero’.

  • [Link Skill] First Step for Unity
  • [Link Skill] Proof of a Brilliant Hero

[Back to Overview]

Maple Union Reorganization

Union ranks will now increase without any additional costs or actions when you reach the matching Union levels.

  • If your Union level increases through things like World Leaps, the Union rank will increase when you log into a character in that world.

[Back to Overview]

V Matrix Reorganization

The V Matrix system has been reorganized.

  • The V Matrix system has been reorganized so that you can acquire V Points through Core Gemstones and use them to enhance your V Cores.
  • You can acquire V Points through Core Gemstones, and you can hold up to 9,999 V Points.
  • You will acquire a certain number of V Points based on the type of Core Gemstone as follows.

※ The ‘Spider in Mirror’ core can be unlocked by using the ‘Mirror World’s Core Gemstone’ 1 time. Subsequent uses will provide V Points.

※ The ‘Crest of the Solar’ core can be unlocked by using the ‘Mitra’s Core Gemstone’ 1 time. Subsequent uses will provide V Points.

  • V Cores have been reclassified as Job Cores, Enhancement Cores, Common Cores, and Special Cores.
    • Job Core: When activated, you can acquire skills exclusive to your job. They can be enhanced up to level 30.
    • Enhancement Core: When activated, you can enhance 4th job and lower, and Hyper skills. They can be enhanced up to level 60.
    • Common Core: When activated, you can acquire skills common to multiple jobs. They can be enhanced up to level 30.
    • Special Core: When activated, you can activate specific effects when you meet special conditions in combat. You can unlock multiple Special Cores, but only 1 can be equipped at a time.

NOTE: If you want to see the amount of required V Points to level up skills, you can check this page on Namu Wiki.

  • Special Cores can be accessed through the button in the top right corner of the V Matrix. They cost 14 V Points to unlock. You can click on an unlocked Special Core to equip it.
  • You can right-click on an unlocked Special Core to extend its duration. The amount of V Points consumed will be based on the duration it is being extended (maximum 48).
  • You can reset your V Point allocation by spending 10m mesos.
  • You can convert 2 V Points into 1 Core Gemstone, which can only be put into storage.
  • The slot enhancement and slot expansion based on character level features have been removed.

The following Special Cores have been removed.

  • Comeback I
  • Auto Recovery I
  • Lucky Recovery I
  • Rune-Frenzied
  • Consecutive Strike I
  • Lethal Strike II
  • Rune-Blessed

Certain Special Cores’ names have been changed as follows.

  • The Auto Recovery II Core has been changed into the Auto Recovery Core.
  • The Comeback II Core has been changed into the Comeback Core.

Existing V Matrix information will be transferred to match the reorganized V Matrix. Existing Core levels will be transferred to V Core growth levels as follows.

  • Job Cores and Skill Cores
    • Equipped Cores will be transferred to match the reorganized V Matrix levels without any separate enhancements.
    • Unequipped Cores will be converted into V Points based on their disassembled value.

※ However, for Kinesis, if all 4 Job Cores are at max level, they will be transferred to max level V Cores. If they are not maxed, they will be converted into V Points based on the number of Core Gemstones required to enhance them.

  • Enhancement Cores
    • Equipped Cores will be converted into V Points based on the number of Core Gemstones required to enhance them.
    • If the Enhancement Cores’ converted V Points are 444 or higher, all Enhancement Cores will be transferred as max level and the extra V Points will be provided.
    • Unequipped Cores will be converted into V Points based on their disassembled value.
  • Special Cores
    • Equipped Special Cores will maintain their expiration date and equipped status.
    • Unequipped Special Cores will maintain their expiration date. However, if you have 2 or more of the same Core in your inventory, only the one with the longest expiration date will be maintained. The remaining Cores will be disassembled and you will receive 1.6 V Points per 1 day past 7 days of their remaining expiration date.
    • Expired Special Cores will be converted into 3 V Points per Core.

※ Decimal values will be rounded up.

  • V Core Fragments
    • V Core Fragments will be converted into 1 V Point per 17.62 V Core Fragments.

※ Decimal values will be rounded up.

  • Condensed Experience Cores
    • Condensed Experience Cores will be converted into 3 V Points per Core.

The following achievements have been moved to the Memories tab.

  • [V Matrix] Constantly Crafting
  • [V Matrix] Crafting Without Breaks
  • [V Matrix] Cores Don’t Betray You
  • [V Matrix] Disassembling to Combine
  • [V Matrix] Core Cutting Warrior
  • [V Matrix] Shattered

[Back to Overview]

Buff Manager and Buff Reorganization

The Buff Manager system has been added.

  • You can click on the Buff Manager button on the Buff Sequence to open the Buff Manager UI.
  • You can check and buy important buff items through the Buff Manager.
  • You can double-click on an item icon in the Buff Manager to buy it.
  • Even if you don’t currently have a buff item, you can register it in a Sequence through the Buff Manager.
  • Items that can be purchased in the Cash Shop can only be bought using Maple Points through this UI.
  • You can double-click on items that can be purchased in the Maple Auction to instantly go to the Maple Auction with that item already searched for.

The Buy Buffs in Bulk feature has been added.

  • You can click the ‘Buy Buffs in Bulk’ button in the Buff Sequence to buy all buffs registered in a Sequence.
  • You can only buy items while clicking on a Sequence, and only the buff items in the clicked Sequence will be bought.

※ Items that can be purchased in the Maple Auction cannot be bought through the Buy Buffs in Bulk feature.

The Buff Items tab has been added to the Maple Auction.

  • You can double-click on an icon in the Buff Items tab to instantly search for the selected item.
  • Double-clicking a buff icon will automatically search for the lowest price.

The Elixir of Honor has been added.

  • Characters level 200 or higher who have completed the ‘[Collector’s Request] quests can buy the Elixir of Honor from the Collector NPC.

※ If a character within the same world has cleared the ‘[Collector’s Request]’ quests, then other characters level 200 or higher can buy the Collector’s Elixirs.

※ The Elixir of Honor can only be put into storage, and is permanent.

※ The Elixir of Honor cannot be used in certain maps, including boss battle maps, Mu Lung Dojo, Sharenian’s Underground Culvert, and event maps.

※ The Boss Practice Mode/Foggy Forest Training Centre Elixir of Honor does not have a separate duration.

The following items’ expiration dates have been removed and they have been changed into permanent items. They can now be put into storage.

  • Alleria’s Elixir
  • Sayram’s Elixir
  • Collector’s Elixir

※ Items with an expiration date of December 18, 2025 at 3:00 AM or earlier will be removed.

You can no longer acquire the Bright Moonlight Potion from the following items.

  • Sealed Box (Hat)
  • Sealed Box (Overall/Top/Bottom)
  • Sealed Box (Shoes/Gloves)
  • Sealed Box (Weapon)
  • Chaos Sealed Box (Hat)
  • Chaos Sealed Box (Overall/Top/Bottom)
  • Chaos Sealed Box (Shoes/Gloves)
  • Chaos Sealed Box (Weapon)

You can now acquire the Bright Moonlight Potion by defeating the following bosses.

  • Chosen Seren (Extreme)
  • Watcher Kalos (Extreme)
  • Kaling (Extreme)
  • First Adversary (Extreme)

You can now use the Champion’s Blessing in Practice Mode.

  • You can only use it if you have a play record in the Champion Raid for this month.
  • The Champion’s Blessing levels available will be based on the following point requirements.

※ For the Practice Mode Champion’s Blessing, all stats (except for experience acquisition) will be applied at the selected Champion’s Blessing stage.

[Back to Overview]

Soul Collection Reorganization

The Soul List feature has been reorganized.

  • You can sort the list by selecting your desired order and conditions using the drop-down box on the left.
  • You can use the Favourites feature to sort your frequently accesed monsters to the top of the list.
  • You can now check the Soul Collection levels for each monster in the list at a glance.
  • You can now view the selected monster on the right side of the UI at the same time as the list.

The Detailed Information feature has been reorganized.

  • You can now check the regular and Magnificent Soul Skills’ information without any additional actions.
  • An indicator has been added for the currently equipped Soul Skill.

Soul Shard items will now be instantly reflected in the Soul Collection UI when acquired.

  • The Soul Shards held by a character will now be displayed in each monster’s page or the Bulk Craft UI.
  • All Soul Shard items previously held in bags, inventory, and the boss reward storage will be automatically reflected in the Soul Collection UI.

※ All held Soul Shard items will be removed after being reflected in the UI.

※ You can hold up to 99,999 Soul Shards for each monster.

※ The Piggy Bar Soul Shards, Pink Mong Soul Shards, and Premium PC Room Soul Shards can be exchanged for Souls by usign the Soul Shards like before.

You can now craft Souls in the Soul Collection UI.

  • You can craft a selected monster’s Soul through the ‘Soul Craft’ button.
  • The ‘Bulk Soul Craft’ feature has been added.
    • The ‘Auto Input’ feature lets you automatically input the Souls you want to craft based on the available space in your bag and inventory.
    • You can reset the inputted Soul quantity.
    • You can check the acquisition rate for each Soul.

The Soul Registration feature has been reorganized.

  • You can register monsters’ Souls through the ‘Soul Register’ button or by dragging and dropping them on to the right side of the UI.
  • The ‘Bulk Soul Register’ feature has been added.
    • You can click on unregistered Souls to register them at once.
    • You can use the ‘Auto Select’ feature to select time-limited untradeable regular Souls all at once.
    • Registration results will be displayed in the chat window, up to a maximum of 5.

※ Magnificent Souls will not be be selected by the ‘Auto Select’ feature.

You can now extract Souls in the Soul Collection UI.

  • You can now extract permanent regular Soul items to acquire Soul Shards without having to consume any additional items.

※ You can select Souls to extract in groups of 5, and the Soul Shards acquired from disassembly may be different from the extracted Souls.

※ You cannot extracted time-limited Souls, Magnificent Souls, and certain other Souls.

※ You can no longer acquire Soul Disassemblers from Gold Apples.

Murmur’s Soul has been added to the Soul Collection.

Cygnus’ normal illustration has been updated in the Soul Collection.

The Full Menu’s ‘Soul Weapon’ option has been changed to ‘Soul Collection’.

A Soul Collection hotkey has been added.

The Soul Weapon UI has been changed.

  • You can now open the Soul Weapon UI from the Soul Collection UI.
  • The Soul Weapon UI design has been changed.

Soul item tooltips will now display if they are already registered in the Soul Collection or not.

The Soul Skill tooltip will now display the Soul Skill usage command.

[Back to Overview]

Item Trade UI Reorganization

The existing ‘Regular Item Trade’ and ‘Cash Item Trade’ features have been unified into one UI that lets you trade any type of item.

The ‘Registration’ process has been added.

  • You can place the items you want to send to the other person, press the ‘Register’ button to complete the registration process, then click the ‘Trade’ button to proceed with the trade.

The ‘View Character Info’ button has been added.

[Back to Overview]

World Meso Unification

Mesos will now be shared between all characters within the same world.

  • After the update, all mesos held by each character within the world will be combined and unified.
  • The storage’s meso storage feature will be maintained.
  • When you World Leap, you can login to a character in the new world and click the ‘Receive World Leap Mesos’ notification to claim your mesos. If you have remaining mesos to claim, the notification will appear each time you login.

The maximum meso hold limit has been changed to 9,999,999,999,999 (~10t mesos).

[Back to Overview]

Voice Chat

The Voice Chat feature has been added that lets you communicate with your party members through voice.

  • You can choose to use Voice Chat or not when creating a party.
  • Parties using Voice Chat can access the Voice Chat settings UI through the party UI.
  • You can adjust microphone sensitivity, microphone and audio volumes through the Voice Chat settings UI.
  • The party leader can start or stop using Voice Chat through the Voice Chat settings UI.
  • The default audio device of the PC you are using MapleStory on will be used as the sound device.
  • When you use the Voice Chat for the first time, there may be delays sometimes in connecting to the sound device.
  • You cannot use or connect to Voice Chat in the following cases.
    • Using a non-default audio device while using multiple PCs at the same time.
    • Chat restriction is applied.
    • Using MapleStory on Mac OS.

You can link your Nexon account and Discord account.

  • You can link your Discord account by using the Options UI’s Chat & Invite tab.
  • If you link your Discord account, you can view your Discord friends list in-game.

[Back to Overview]

MapleStory Collection Cards

The <MapleStory Collection Cards> have been released.

  • You can collect cards featuring the stunning appearances of MapleStory classes and enjoy your personal collection in-game.
  • For MapleStory Collection Card sale and product information, please check the separate information page.

NOTE: You can see all 152 cards in this YouTube video.

Registering Cards

  • You can scan the QR code on the back of a Collection Card using the MapleHands+ app to register the card.

※ You can only register 1 Collection Card of each type. You cannot register duplicate cards of the same type.

※ 1 Collection Card can only be reigstered 1 time. Already registered cards cannot be registered again.

※ Registered Collection Cards are saved per Maple ID.

※ You can only register MapleStory Collection Cards CLASS EDITION until December 31, 2026.

Checking In-Game

  • When you register a card, you can start the ‘[Collection Card] Magician Cootie’s Call’ quest.
  • After completing all related quests, you will acquire the ‘Collection Card Binder’ furniture. You can install it in your Maple Home and use it to open the dedicated UI where you can check your Collection Cards.
  • Other players can visit your home and use the ‘Collection Card Binder’ to check your collection.

※ Quest progress is shared between all worlds, and can only be completed once per Maple ID.

※ Maple Home requires a level 260 or higher character that has completed ‘The Day After’ story within the world. You can then complete the ‘[Maple Home] Spiegelmann’s Super Gift’ quest through the lightbulb icon to create a Maple Home.

※ The ‘Collection Card Binder’ furniture can be used by all characters within the same Maple ID.

Collection Card Rewards

  • When you register a cumulative number of cards and meet certain conditions, you can receive the following rewards.

※ The rewards can only be claimed once per Maple ID, regardless of world.

[Back to Overview]

Custom Illustrations

Custom illustrations based on your own unique outfits! Save your illustrations and apply them you desire when viewing stories or looking at the character info window!

Custom Illustration Introduction

  • The Custom Illustration system uses generative AI to transform your character’s appearance into an illustration in MapleStory’s art style.
  • Custom illustrations will be operated as a beta service after the update.
  • To use custom illustrations, you need an ‘Illustration Request Ticket’ item. For detailed instructions on how to acquire them, please check the ‘Custom Illustration Event’ section.
  • Accounts with a level 270 or higher character will be entered automatically in the pre-request event. You can check the results immediately after logging in during the event period. For more information, please check the ‘Custom Illustration Pre-Request Event’ page.

How to Request an Illustration

  • After customizing your character with your desired appearance, you can click the Custom Illustration button in the character info window, use the Illustration Request Ticket, or talk to the Moving Easel NPC to open the Custom Illustration window.
  • If you have an Illustration Request Ticket, you can click the ‘Request an Illustration’ button to start the request.
  • After a certain amount of time has passed after your request, a notification will appear that opens the Custom Illustration UI where you can check your request results.
  • You can select all of the illustrations that you wish to save from the request results and click ‘Finish’ to complete the request.

Applying Illustrations

  • You can select and apply one of your saved illustrations from the Custom Illustration window.
  • You can set each illustration’s background colour to either a solid colour or a gradient.
  • The currently applied illustration will appear in the story dialogue windows instead of your character sprite and job illustration.
  • The currently applied illustration will also be displayed in the Custom Illustration window in the Character Info UI. You can change the settings to display this or not when other characters view your character’s info.
  • You can change the custom illustration’s application range through the Custom Illustration UI’s options.
    • Display in Story Dialogue
    • Share with Other Characters
    • View Other Characters’ Illustrations

Notes When Requesting Illustrations

  • The following things will not be included in the requested appearance.
    • Weapon and secondary weapon items
    • Effect items, Cash effects, weapon effects
    • Character’s actions, motions, and emotes
    • Character’s mounts and chairs
    • Transformations and Illusion Rings
  • You cannot complete a request while wearing a collaboration item, items that have poor results, or items that may have inappropriate results. Please follow the instructions when requesting.
  • An illustration request’s results can be viewed for about 7 days after requesting. However, if the request is not completed within that period, it will expire and can no longer be saved. Please make sure to save them within the period.
  • If you have an activate illustration request on another character in your account, you cannot proceed with another request on a different character at the same time.
  • For Zero characters, the custom illustration storage slots are separate for Alpha and Beta. The request results can only be saved on the character that submitted the request. In addition, each Alpha and Beta character is assigned a separate custom illustration, so every time the character is Tagged, the corresponding custom illustration will be applied.
  • Custom illustrations will not be applied in the story dialogue windows in certain Zero class stories.
  • For Angelic Buster characters, the illustrations can be applied to both Normal and Dress-Up Mode. The corresponding custom illustration will be applied based on the character’s Mode.
  • If you World Leap a character while a request is in-progress, the request resutls will disappear. Please save the request results before World Leaping.
  • The saved illustrations are stored per character, and if the character is deleted, the saved illustrations will also be deleted.

※ Please understand that the list of items that cannot be included in a request may change in real time.

※ The illustration results are created using generative AI, so the quality and elements of each results may vary.

[Back to Overview]

Cash

The MVP purchase amount criteria has been changed from monthly to weekly.

  • The MVP system’s rank calculation criteria has been changed from ‘purchase amount for the past 3 months, including the current month’ to ‘purchase amount for the past 13 weeks, including the current week’.

※ ‘1 week’ from the above refers to the period from Thursday at 12:00 AM until the following Wednesday at 11:59 PM.

  • For players who have already reached MVP ranks between October 2025~December 2025, their weekly purchase amounts will be adjusted as follows to ensure that their MVP benefits are not applied for a shorter period than before the update.
    • Purchase amounts from October 1, 2025 will be combined with the purchase amounts from October 2~8, 2025.
    • Purchase amounts from November 1~5, 2025 will be combined with the purchase amounts from November 6~12, 2025.
    • Purchase amounts from December 1~3, 2025 will be combined with the purchase amounts from December 4~10, 2025.

※ After the December 18 maintenance, MVP ranks will be reset based on the above criteria.

※ Example:

The MVP monthly benefits have been changed into weekly benefits.

  • Sol Erda
    • This will now be provided as Faint Sol Erda Energy.

※ The Faint Sol Erda Energy will expire after 30 days.

  • MVP Resort
    • All unused usage time will no longer be reset, and will now accumulate up to a maximum of 90 hours.

※ If the total amount of added time and remaining MVP Resort time exceeds 90 hours, it will only accumulate up to 90 hours. (Excess remaining time will not be accumulated again).

※ Any remaining MVP Resort usage time that was unused before the December 18 maintenance will be maintained.

  • Kinship Ring/EXP Boost Ring (15%)
    • The acquired items will now expire on the following Thursday at 12:00 AM.
    • The items can no longer be moved to the Cash Storage.
  • Spirit Pendant
    • The acquired items will now expire on the following Thursday at 12:00 AM.
  • MVP Infinite Elixir/MVP Basic Damage Skin (Unit)
    • The items will longer be removed if you maintain your MVP rank at Bronze or higher.
  • MVP Title/MVP Medal/MVP Chair/MVP Nametag Ring/MVP Chat Bubble Ring
    • The items will longer be removed if you maintain your MVP rank.

※ Monthly benefit items acquired before the December 18 maintenance will maintain their original specifications.

※ For any monthly benefit items that were not acquired before the December 18 maintenance, you can talk to the Maple Admin NPC until December 31, 2025 at 11:59 PM to acquire the Sol Erda, Kinship Ring, Experience Boost Ring (15%), and Spirit Pendant. The items will expire on December 31, 2025 at 11:59 PM.

The MapleStory All Jobs Illustration Collection will be available for purchase.

  • Sale Duration: December 18, 2025 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
  • All Jobs’ Illustration Collection Outfit Packages and the All Jobs Illustration Collection Hair Coupon will be available for purchase.
    • All Jobs Illustration Collection Hair Coupon
      • Sale Location: Cash Shop > Beauty > Hair
    • Outfit Packages
      • Sale Location: Cash Shop > Seasonal Sales > All Jobs Collection
    • Newly Available
      • Friends World Jobs’ Outfit Packages
      • Transcendent Jobs’ Outfit Packages
      • Nova Jobs’ Outfit Packages
      • Resistance Jobs’ Outfit Packages
      • Demon Jobs’ Outfit Packages
      • Lef Jobs’ Outfit Packages
      • Anima Jobs’ Outfit Packages
    • Also Available
      • Adventurer Jobs’ Outfit Packages
      • Cygnus Knights Jobs’ Outfit Packages
      • Hero Jobs’ Outfit Packages

NOTE: You can see all of the options here:

  • All Jobs Illustration Collection Hair Coupon
  • Adventurers
  • Cygnus Knights & Heroes
  • Demons & Resistance
  • Nova & Lef
  • Friends World & Transcendent & Anima

The MapleStory Boss Collection will be available for purchase.

  • Sale Duration: December 18, 2025 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
  • The Boss Concept Outfit Packages, Weapons, Boss Hair, and Boss Face Coupons will be available for purchase.
    • Sale Location: Cash Shop > Seasonal Sales > Boss Collection
    • Newly Available
      • First Adversary Package
      • First Adversary’s Weapons
      • Baldrix Package
    • Also Available
      • Magnus Package
      • Arkarium Package
      • Dark Cygnus Package
      • Damien Package
      • Will Package
      • Jin Hilla Package
      • Dunkel Package
      • Black Mage Package
      • Chosen Seren Package
      • Kaling Package
      • Extreme Bigfoot Package
      • Limbo Package

NOTE: You can see all of the options here.

The All Jobs Hair/Face Coupons will be available for purchase.

  • Sale Duration: December 18, 2025 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
  • Beauty coupons for all jobs’ default hairs/faces will be available for purchase.
    • Hair Coupon Sale Location: Cash Shop > Beauty > Hair > All Jobs Hair Coupon
    • Face Coupon Sale Location: Cash Shop > Beauty > Face > All Jobs Face Coupon

NOTE: You can see all of the options here.

New Cash items will be available for purchase.

※ These items can also be purchased entirely with Mileage.

The Mashoor’s Gifts Weather Effect and 10 MVP Superpower Buffs Voucher will no longer be available to purchase in the Mileage Shop.

The following Cash items’ purchase limit per Maple ID have been reset.

  • Welcome to Maple Package
  • Styling Starter Package

※ Maple IDs that purchased these packages before the December 18 update can also purchase them again.

Certain changes have been made to the Wisp’s Wonderberry usage cutscene.

The limit on the number of items with the Colouring Prism/Colouring Prism Pro/Weapon Effect Prism applied that could be equipped has been removed.

The changed colours of items with the Colouring Prism/Colouring Prism Pro/Weapon Effect Prism applied will no longer be displayed on MapleHands+, the homepage, and certain content UI that display character appearances.

Developer Notes:

We have confirmed that it will take more time to resolve the server load issues caused by Colouring Prisms/Colouring Prism Pro/Weapon Effect Prisms, so in this update, we have changed it so that you can equip any number of items with Prisms applied with no more restrictions. However, instead of that, we have limited the display of changed colours from Prisms in certain content.

This is a temporary measure, and we will strive to resolve the root cause of the server load issue as quickly as possible to restore normal usage of Prism items.

We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience caused by this inability to provide a stable service.

[Back to Overview]

Skill Changes

Common

  • Link Skills’ passive effects will no longer affect Ascent skills’ damage.
  • Fixed an issue where Link Skills could be acquired below level 70.
  • Fixed an issue where certain skills that reduced % HP attacks’ damage to party members did not display the reduced damage. The changed skills are as follows.
    • Pathfinder – Obsidian Barrier
    • Shadower – Smokescreen
    • Battle Mage – Shelter
  • Fixed an issue where if you right-clicked Freud’s Wisdom at 6 stacks to c ancel it, the cooldown would be applied again.
  • Fixed an issue where skill cast actions were awkward when using certain floating skills.

NOTE: Almost all of the skill changes in this patch were to each job’s Ascent skill to account for the Link Skill passive change above. I’m not going to list them all since it doesn’t really matter that much, but if you want to check them out, you can check the original patch notes yourself.

Bowmaster

  • Arrow Platter
    • Fixed an issue where you could not install this skill with the NPC Chat/Harvest key in Capture Mode.
  • Arrow Platter VI
    • Fixed an issue where you could not install this skill with the NPC Chat/Harvest key in Capture Mode.

Night Lord

  • Life and Death
    • Fixed an issue where sometimes the damage was not applied properly.

Soul Master

  • Rising Sun
    • Fixed an issue where the activation state was not maintained.
  • Falling Moon
    • Fixed an issue where the activation state was not maintained.
  • Soluna Time
    • Fixed an issue where after learning Master of the Sword, Soluna Time’s effects applied when using Decent Combat Orders/Combat Orders/Collector’s Elixir would sometimes be maintained even after the duration ended.

Wild Hunter

  • Fixed an issue where basic attacking while riding the Jaguar would look awkward.

Mechanic

  • After completing certain content, you will now re-mount your Metal Armor. This will apply to the following content.
    • Epic Dungeon: Angler Company’s Stage 3/7 when heading to the security area.
    • Epic Dungeon: Nightmare Paradise’s Stage 4 when riding the flying cloud.
  • After completing certain content, Robot Transition: CB-P1 will now be reactivated. This will apply to the following content.
    • Epic Dungeon: High Mountain when transforming into Mitra.
    • Kaoong when hit by the shrink ray.
    • Arkarium (Easy/Normal) when transformed into a snake.
    • Cygnus (Easy/Normal) when transformed into a Ribbon Pig.
    • Cygnus (Easy/Normal) when hit by the Abyss Tornado.

Demon Avenger

  • Demon Frenzy
    • The recovery item and skills’ recovery amount limit has been decreased from 2% to 1% max HP.
    • A new skill, ‘Frenzy Break‘ has been added.
    • While Demon Frenzy is active, you can switch to Frenzy Break. While in Frenzy Break, the recovery item and skills’ recovery amount limit will be increased to 2% max HP.

Khali

  • Fixed an issue where certain skill effects looked awkward. The changed skills are as follows.
    • Void Rush
    • Void Blitz
    • Hex: Chakram Split
    • Hex: Pandemonium
    • Void Rush VI
    • Void Blitz VI
    • Hex: Chakram Split VI
    • Void Awake

Len

  • Fixed an issue where notifications for certain skills would sometimes still appear even if they were removed or turned off after being registered. The changed skills are as follows.
    • Plum Blossom Sword 3rd Form: Single Strike Keen Blade
    • Plum Blossom Sword 3rd Form: Single Strike Keen Blade VI

[Back to Overview]

Improvements

Quests

The 6th job advancement quests’ difficulty has been alleviated.

  • It has been improved so that the 6 Symbol Recovery quests no longer require you to charge 6 stones before you can proceed with them.
  • The Symbol Recovery mission path has been simplified, allowing you to continue the quests immediately without having to talk to the Goddess NPCs.
  • The Road of Vanishing Symbol Recovery quest difficulty has been alleviated. The Unstable Giant Erdas summon cycle has been shortened, and the damage dealt to monsters by the Condenser has been increased.
  • In the Arcana Symbol Recovery quest, you can now rescue the Rock Spirits by using the NPC Chat/Harvest key.
  • A cutscene skip feature has been applied. You can click the button the top right corner of the screen to use it.

※ Any held Road of Vanishing/Chew Chew Island/Lacheln/Arcana/Morass/Esfera Stones held will be taken back. Characters who were currently charging the stones can also instantly proceed with the quests.

When you complete the ‘[6th Job] New Power’ quest, your HP and MP will be fully recovered.

Fixed an issue where players could enter the Suspicious Cave Path map without accepting the ‘[Resistance] Find the Missing Job Instructor’ quest.

Certain Authentic Force related quests’ details have been changed.

  • A new cutscene has been added to the ‘[Cernium] Another Power, Authentic Force’ quest.
  • Certain dialogue in the ‘[Arteria] To the Front Lines’ quest has been changed.
  • Certain dialogue in the ‘[Tallahart] Resolve’ quest has been changed.
  • The initial accept dialogue for the ‘[Daily Quest] Tallahart Ancient God’s Power Investigation’ quest has been changed.
  • Certain details in Maple Chronicle ‘Book of Grandis – Arteria’ have been changed.

[Back to Overview]

Content

Certain content’s experience provided to level 270~272 characters has been increased. The changed content are as follows.

  • Advanced EXP Vouchers
  • VIP Sauna
  • MVP Resort

[Back to Overview]

Monsters

The Tallahart Elite Boss ‘Dark Thought’ can now be hit while teleporting.

[Back to Overview]

Items

The following char items’ descriptions have been changed to be more clear.

  • Discovery Campfire Chair
  • Twin Stroller Chair
  • Duel of Light and Lightning
  • Ignition’s Judgement

Growth Potion item names will now display the levels they can be used.

2 new bag items have been added.

  • 80 Slot Chair Bag
  • 80 Slot Title Wallet

Item effects will now be displayed in certain situations such as the character info window and Maple Chat.

Zero characters can now equip regular equipment with the Free Style Coupon applied.

Item set effects will now be displayed for Syncroid and Unionroid.

The names of the Adventurer job hairs in the All Jobs Illustration Collection Hair Coupon will now display ‘Adventurer’ at the start.

[Back to Overview]

Maps

Fixed an issue where the number of remaining monsters notification message would not appear repeatedly when defeating monsters with multikills in Monster Park Beginner/Intermediate dungeons.

Fixed an issue where characters with Safe Mode enabled would have it removed if they were forcefully moved to a map where Safe Mode was not available.

Fixed an issue where pets and Androids were not reflected in water.

[Back to Overview]

UI

The Hyper Stat preset change cost has been removed.

You can no longer change Hyper Stat presets in boss battles.

The character right-click menu has been reorganized.

  • The design has been changed.
  • Certain menu locations have been changed.

Reserve Special Skill Rings can now be disassembled or synthesized after being unequipped.

If you are chat restricted, you can no longer change the party description in the party recruitment board.

You can now register Special Core skills in the Buff Bookmarks.

  • The currently equipped Special Core skill will now be displayed in the skill window. However, expired Special Core skills will not be displayed.
  • Special Core skills displayed in the skill window can now be registered in the Buff Bookmarks.

It has been improved so that when a skill is registered in the Buff Bookmarks, it will no longer be removed from all Buff Bookmarks.

The Quest Postbox, accessed through the lightbulb icon on the left side of the screen, has been reorganized.

  • The design has been changed.
  • A checkbox has been added that lets you quickly look at your desired quests.
  • A button to access the quest information window has been added.

The Quest Info window has been reorganized.

  • Multiple checkboxes in the Story category can now be selected at the same time.
  • The ‘All’ and ‘Event’ checkboxes have been added to the Story category’s checkboxes.
  • The ‘Required’ and ‘Job Advancement’ checkboxes in the Story category have been unified into the ‘Job’ checkbox.
  • The ‘Content’ and ‘System’ checkboxes in the Story category have been unified into the ‘Content/System’ checkbox.
  • The quest information window’s quest progress related button size has been increased.
  • When searching for quests in the Story category, if no results are found within the selected checkboxes, the search will now be done over all Story category quests.

※ The Story category’s job advancement quest image tag has been changed from ‘Job’ to ‘Job Advancement’. They will now be displayed at the top of the job quests.

When searching for items in bags or storage in your inventory, the item location will now be displayed next to the item name.

When opening a bag in your inventory, the bag will no longer be covered by other UI.

[Back to Overview]

Security

The Macro Detection System (AI-Generated Lie Detector) activation range has been expanded.

  • It can now also be activated while grinding.

[Back to Overview]

Error Fixes

Quests

  • Fixed an issue with the ‘[Evan] Dangerious Premonition’ quest where the monsters appeared awkwardly.
  • Fixed an issue with the ‘[Mushroom Castle] In Search of the Princess 1’ where the dialogue was awkwardly displayed in certain situations.
  • Fixed an issue with the ‘[5th Job] Grandis’ Arcane Stone’ quest where the dialogue was awkward when playing as an Illium.

[Back to Overview]

Items

  • Fixed an issue where certain Cash items’ effects did not disappear when interacting with furniture in Maple Home.
  • Fixed an issue where rechargeable items could sometimes not be put in the storage.
  • Fixed an issue where Decent Skills acquired by wearing equipment items sometimes were not refreshed instantly.
  • Fixed an issue where Combat Power comparison between Zero weapons was sometimes incorrect.
  • Fixed an issue where characters with the Burning Beyond effect applied could use Extreme Growth Potions.
  • When you reach the weekly boss clear limit, you can no longer use Empress/Hard Magnus/Chaos Von Bon/Chaos Pierre/Chaos Bloody Queen/Chaos Vellum Clear Count Reset Tickets.

[Back to Overview]

NPCs

  • Fixed an issue where the NPC Baban did not appear for characters who cleared the Black Heaven content which made it impossible to complete the ‘[Leben Mine] Wonny’s Suffering’ quest.

[Back to Overview]

Monsters

  • Fixed an issue where sometimes Damage Skins were displayed awkwardly when applying a bind to certain monsters.
  • Fixed an issue where sometimes the debuff effect would be displayed awkwardly when applying a bind to certain monsters.
  • Fixed an issue in Azmoth Canyon (Normal/Hard) where if you applied a bind to Kind Azrael, sometimes it would move.
  • Fixed an issue in Chosen Seren (Normal/Hard/Extreme) Phase 2 Sunset where if you used certain cleanse skills, the burn debuff’s heal block would not be removed. The changed skills are as follows.
    • Bishop’s Dispel
    • Battle Mage’s Blue Aura with Blue Aura- Dispel Magic applied, Union Aura
    • Zero’s Time Distortion
    • Lara’s Arbor Away
  • Fixed an issue in Limbo (Normal/Hard) Phase 3 where if you defeated Limbo at the same time as the Thought Frenzy, sometimes 2 Limbos would appear.
  • Fixed an issue in where if the last monster died in Cernium/Arcs/Odium area mOnster Parks, the effect would be awkward.
  • Fixed an issue in Epic Dungeon: Nightmare Paradise Stage 4 where if a Zero character tagged while riding the flying cloud, the map would look awkward.

[Back to Overview]

Maps

  • Fixed an issue where certain objects in the Secret Plaza map were displayed awkwardly.
  • Fixed an issue where the card objects in the Mirror World Ludibrium Toy Factory map were displayed awkwardly.

[Back to Overview]

UI

  • Fixed an issue where the game would crash in certain situations if you registered Noblesse guild skills in a Buff Sequence when you could not use them then used the Sequence.
  • Fixed an issue with the Enhancement UI where if you used a special scroll that increased a fixed value successfully, the increased value would not be displayed.
  • Fixed an issue where the Master of Enhancements/Crafting effect tooltip descriptions were awkward.
  • Fixed an issue where sometimes the client would crash if you were viewing equipment item tooltips in a shop or Maple Auction and pressed the NPC Chat/Harvest key to switch to the detailed tooltip information after the item’s duration ended or the item was sold.
  • Fixed an issue where if the Archive hotkey was already registered in the hotkey settings, sometimes it would loo awkward.
  • Fixed an issue where Evan’s dragon was not transparent even when other characters’ skill effect transparency was set to the lowest level.
  • Fixed an issue where the weekly clear limit notification would not be displayed when focusing on a weekly boss using the arrow keys in the Boss Entry UI.
  • Fixed an issue where the inventory search window’s search list scroll image was displayed awkwardly.
  • Fixed an issue where the 6th job advancement quest/symbol recovery missions where the timer and progress UIs overlapped.
  • The spacing and spelling of some text in item tooltips in the Cash Shop have been corrected to the proper spelling.

[Back to Overview]

Events

Challengers World Season 3

Challengers World

Fast growth! Abundant benefits! Up to 3 Tera Burning characters! They await you in Challengers World.

Event Participation Requirements:

  • Newly created characters in Challengers, Challengers 2, Challengers 3, or Challengers 4 world.

Event Period:

  • Growth Period: December 18, 2025 after the maintenance ~ April 16, 2026 before the maintenance.
  • Challengers World Leap Period
    • 1st Early Leap Period: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
    • 2nd Early Leap Period: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
    • 3rd Early Leap Period: March 19, 2026 after the maintenance ~ April 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
    • End Leap Period: April 16, 2026 after the maintenance ~ April 29, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

※ You can World Leap to all worlds.

※ You can only move to the first world you selected as the arrival world. Afterwards, you cannot change that world.

※ You can use World Leap during the Early World Leap by talking to the NPC Asaga in Henesys.

※ You can Challengers World Leap up to a total of 5 characters to other worlds.

Challengers World Benefits

All characters in Challengers World will receive the special [Challengers Beginner] skill while they are in the world. In addition, based on the Tier you reach from Challengers Missions, the skill will grow step-by-step.

In Challengers World, you can create up to 3 Tera Burning characters. You can create a Zero character if you have a level 100 or higher character in Challengers World. Characters designated as Tera Burning characters will receive the following benefits:

  • +2 levels for each level up, up to level 200
  • Snail pet (30 days)
  • Level 30 Equipment Box
  • Legendary Secret Box
    • Black Hat
    • Black Cape
    • Black Suit
    • Black Weapon Box
    • Black Secondary Weapon Box
  • Expiring Root Abyss Set Box
  • Eternal Flame Title

※ Hyper Burning and regular characters created in Challengers World cannot claim these rewards.

Characters created in Challengers World can receive the following character creation gifts through the Event List.

  • 10th Anniversary White Armor Set Box (7 Days)
  • 1,000 Power Elixirs
  • 1,000 Scroll Traces
  • Equip/Use/Etc./Set-up inventories expanded to 64 slots

※ These rewards can only be received up to 5 times, regardless of Burning status or character level.

In Challengers World, you can use the Meso Market by talking to the NPC Miss Brainy.

In Challengers World, the Maple Auction sale registration and Meso Market usage restriction until 3 days after a character was created has been removed.

※ Characters with active Meso Market transactions cannot proceed with the Challengers World Leap.

※ The Meso Market can be used until the Challengers World growth period ends. (April 16, 2026 before the maintenance)

※ After the growth period ends, you can no longer use the Meso Market. So if you have any mesos/Maple Points you are trading, please make sure to complete the transactions before the April 16, 2026 maintenance.

Event Details

You can only use one world out of Challengers, Challengers 2, Challengers 3, and Challengers 4, whichever one you first create a character on. Deleting the character will not change the world you can use. You can create up to 5 characters.

You can only use the World Leap on characters level 101 or higher.

※ Zero characters can World Leap if they are level 101 or higher and have completed the story quests up to Chapter 2.

※ The Tera Burning effect applied to characters created in Challengers World will not be transferred with the World Leap.

When the World Leap period in Challengers World ends, all characters remaining in the world will be deleted along with their items and Cash items.

Items in your Cash storage, item storage, or Maple Auction will not be moved when you use the World Leap.

The Custom Backgrounds registered in the Custom Backgrounds list will not be moved.

In Challengers World, you cannot purchase the following items from the Cash Shop:

  • Character Slot Expansion Coupon
  • 4/8 Slot Storage Expansion Coupon
  • House Wedding Ticket

The following features are not available in Challengers World:

  • Transferring Link Skills
  • Maple Union
  • Union Artifact System
  • Monster Collection
  • Character Slot Expansions

※ When the growth period ends, you will no longer be able to use shops, storage, or any content other than the World Leap. Make sure you check the ‘Challengers World Benefits’ tab’s information in the Event List.

※ The Challengers World growth period, World Leap duration and target worlds are subject to change.

[Back to Overview]

New Season Boss: Kai

Kai, the time wanderer who wields the ‘Sequencer’, a sword imbued with the power of time. He uses devastating swordsmanship skills. Prove your qualifications over the true power of time by facing the undisputed champion of another world!

The Season Boss, Kai, has been added.

  • This boss can only be challenged in Challengers World.
  • You can only clear this boss 1 time per week per Maple ID. The clear record is reset every Thursday at 12:00 AM.
  • This boss is not included in the weekly boss clear limit.
  • You can acquire more powerful rewards than normal bosses.
  • You will not acquire an Intense Power Crystal when you defeat this boss.

※ Certain rewards can only be used in Challengers World.

Participation Requirements

  • Normal Mode: Level 270 or higher Challengers World character.
  • Hard Mode: Level 280 or higher Challengers World character.

※ Zero can participate after completing story quest Chapter 2.

Participation Method

  • You can enter through the NPC Annette, located in Henesys, Nameless Town, Cernium Square, and City of Researchers in Challengers World.

You can acquire the following rewards by defeating Kai.

  • Gold Meso Pouch
  • Unstable Time Fragment (can be traded, expires on April 16, 2026 at 2:00 AM) (Hard Mode only)
  • Time’s Eternal Armor Box (untradeable, expires after 7 days) (Hard Mode only)
  • Kai’s Eternal Armor Box (untradeable, expires after 7 days) (Hard Mode only)
  • Kai’s Pitch Black Accessory Box (untradeable, expires after 7 days)
  • Kai’s Basic Sol Erda Fragment Box (untradeable, expires after 7 days)
  • Kai’s Advanced Sol Erda Fragment Box (untradeable, expires after 7 days) (Hard Mode only)

※ When you acquire a Gold Meso Pouch, it will instantly increase your held mesos by 10m.

※ You can exchange 10 Unstable Time Fragments for 1 Eternal Hat, Top, Bottom, or Shoulder.

※ You can acquire 1 Eternal Hat, Top, Bottom, or Shoulder from the Time’s Eternal Armor Box.

※ You can acquire 1 Eternal Shoes, Gloves, or Cape from the Kai’s Eternal Armor Box.

※ You can acquire a certain number of untradeable Sol Erda Fragments based on a set rate from the Kai’s Basic/Advanced Sol Erda Fragment Box.

Kai’s Soul Skill has been added.

  • Recall Strike
    [Master Level: 1]
    Kai, summoned by the Soul Weapon’s power, deals a powerful sword strike on enemies in front of you.
    [Activation Command: ↓↓↑↑ + Attack key]
    • [Level 1]
      Consumes 250 Soul. For 120 seconds, summon Kai that deals 450% damage 4 times on up to 10 enemies.
      The Soul Weapon summon’s damage is calculated differently than the character’s.
      Cooldown: 150 seconds.

Information

  • Level: 270 (Normal)/Hard (280)
  • HP: 126t (Normal)/483t (Hard)
  • Time Limit: 20 minutes
  • Death Count: 5

There are a few main mechanics when fighting Kai.

  • While fighting Kai, the Chrono Gauge (top half of the UI) will fill automatically. However, it will be decreased if you are hit by Kai’s attacks.
  • You can use the Chrono Step skill (bottom half of the UI) with the NPC Chat/Harvest key. When used, you will dash in a direction while avoiding Kai’s attacks for 0.5 seconds. You prepare 1 charge every 5 seconds and can hold up to 3 charges.
  • Above the UI, there will be a timer for the Time Distortion. After 1 minute, Kai will activate the Time Distortion where he uses extremely fast skills. When this happens, you can use the Chrono Break skill with the Tab key to slow down time (the duration is based on how full the Chrono Gauge was), allowing you to dodge the attacks with Chrono Step. In the Time Distortion, successfully parrying an attack with Chrono Step will instantly refund its cooldown.
  • As you dodge attacks in Time Distortion, there will be a score at the top of the screen. It will go up as you dodge attacks but if you die during the Time Distortion, it will freeze until you revive. If you can reach 10,000 points before the Time Distortion ends, then Kai will enter a groggy state and all of your cooldowns will be decreased by 40s.
  • When Kai reaches 50% HP, he will become stronger and his skills will be enhanced.

[Back to Overview]

[Boss Clear Event] Kai First Clear Event

The swordsman using the power of time, Kai! Win in a battle against him to acquire various rewards!

Event Participation Requirements:

  • Level 270 or higher characters. Or a Zero character that has completed Chapter 2.

※ Normal Kai’s entry limit is level 270. Hard Kai’s entry limit is level 280.

Event Period:

  • Clear Mission: December 18, 2025 after the maintenance ~ April 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
  • First Clear Mission: December 18, 2025 after the maintenance ~ January 14, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

Event Reward Claim Period:

  • Clear Mission: December 18, 2025 after the maintenance ~ April 16, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
